steps.title: Identify what the student’s goal requires
steps.content:
The student wants to “specify when al-Farisi published his landmass theory.” The key word is when — the answer must state the publication date (1037 CE) from the notes. Any choice that omits the date fails the goal.
  • "In 1037 CE, al-Farisi published his theory that a large landmass existed west of Europe and east of Asia.": Opens with “In 1037 CE,” directly specifying when the theory was published. ✓
  • "Al-Farisi, who studied Earth's physical features, published a theory about a large landmass.": Mentions publication but omits the year 1037 CE, so the “when” is never specified. ✗
  • "Al-Farisi was an Iranian scholar who studied Earth's physical features.": Describes al-Farisi’s field of study; says nothing about his landmass theory or its publication date. ✗
  • "An Iranian scholar who studied Earth's physical features, al-Farisi theorized that a large landmass existed west of Europe and east of Asia.": Describes the content of the theory but omits the publication year entirely. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the correct choice
steps.content: Only In 1037 CE, al-Farisi published his theory that a large landmass existed west of Europe and east of Asia. opens with the specific year the student needs to “specify when” the theory was published. That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
